zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Porter於1624年3月15日出生在英國艾可費爾斯泰德時，她的父親John Porter of Windsor Connecticut29歲，母親Anna White23歲。她於1644年10月24日與Joseph Judson結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1696年3月16日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州費艾費爾德龐培勞格 普藍塔迪享年72歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州費艾費爾德史達科特笛卡爾 從雷加納爾 波英 格洛德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Porter于1624年3月15日出生在英国艾可費爾斯泰德时，她的父亲John Porter of Windsor Connecticut29岁，母亲Anna White23岁。她于1644年10月24日与Joseph Judson结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和8个女儿的父母。她去世于1696年3月16日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州費艾費爾德龐培勞格 普藍塔迪享年72岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州費艾費爾德史達科特笛卡爾 從雷加納爾 波英 格洛德。

nameMeanings.name: Porter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porterfield
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English porter(e) ‘gatekeeper, doorkeeper’ + feld ‘open country, bounded piece of land’, referring to the hereditary lands owned by a porter of a monastery (see Porter 1), taken as a surname by the descendants of such an official. Black suggests that they would originally have been known as Porter but extended their name when ‘territorial surnames’ became fashionable.

variants.name: Sluiter
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a porter or gatekeeper, Middle Dutch sluter. Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Port
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Fortner
meanings: German: variant (also Förtner) or Americanized form of Pförtner, an occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper in a great house, castle, or monastery, Middle High German phortenaere (from Latin porta ‘door, gate’). Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Portier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clovis, Leonce, Renauld.
French: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, Old French portier (from Late Latin portarius). Compare English Porter .
History: Alexandre-Bernard Portier from Paris, France, married Catherine Monte in Edgard, LA, in 1741.

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.description: Oldest grave seen in the memorials list.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

The founders of Woodbury came from Stratford, Connecticut, in the early 1670s. Ancient Woodbury consisted of the present towns of Woodbury, Southbury, Roxbury, Bethlehem, most of Washington and parts of Middlebury and Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Porter was born on 15 March 1624, in Felsted, Essex, England, her father, John Porter of Windsor Connecticut, was 29 and her mother, Anna White, was 23. She married Joseph Judson on 24 October 1644. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 16 March 1696, in Pomperaug Plantation, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 72, and was buried in Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut, United States.

Elle a épousé Joseph Judson le 24 octobre 1644. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 8 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 mars 1696, à Pomperaug Plantation, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterrée à Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Porter è nata il il 15 marzo 1624 nel Felsted, Essex, England, sua padre, John Porter of Windsor Connecticut, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Anna White, aveva 23 anni. ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Porterが1624年3月15日にイギリスエセックスフエルステドで生まれた時，父John Porter of Windsor Connecticutは29歳，母Anna Whiteは23歳であった。1644年10月24日にJoseph Judsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1696年3月16日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ファイルフィールドポンペラウグ プランタティオンで72歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ファイルフィールドストラトフォードオルド コングレガティオナル ブリイング グロウンドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Porter(이)가 1624년 3월 15일 영국 에세스 프스테드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Porter of Windsor Connecticut(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna White(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1644년 10월 24일 Joseph Judson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1696년 3월 16일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 파어필드 폼페라그 플랜타티언에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 파어필드 스트라트퍼드 드 콘그레가티오널 버이잉 그라운드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Porter nasceu em 15 de março de 1624, em Felsted, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Porter of Windsor Connecticut, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Anna White, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Joseph Judson em 24 de outubro de 1644. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de março de 1696, em Pomperaug Plantation, Fairfield, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 72 anos, e foi sepultada em Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Porter родилась 15 марта 1624 г. в местности фелстед, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени John Porter of Windsor Connecticut было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna White, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Joseph Judson 24 октября 1644 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 8 дочерей. Она умерла 16 марта 1696 г. в местности помпирауг плантатион, феирфиелд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 72 лет и была похоронена в местности олд конгрегатиональ буринг гроунд, стратфорд, феирфиелд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sarah Porter nació el 15 de marzo de 1624, en Felsted, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, John Porter of Windsor Connecticut, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Anna White, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Joseph Judson el 24 de octubre de 1644. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 16 de marzo de 1696, en Pomperaug Plantation, Fairfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultada en Old Congregational Burying Ground, Stratford, Fairfield, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
